user_prompt: Implement `lib\AudioFileRecorder\AudioFileDecoder.cpp` and its associated header file. It should be a class object
Reference `file_packet_t`, `lib\AudioFileRecorder\AudioFileRecorder.cpp`, `scripts\thefly_audio_decoder.py`
The constructor dictates what the input file name is, and registers callbacks
- on complete
- on progress
The class itself has public functions for
- start
- cancel
- poll
- getting the state
- getting the progress
- the destructor must clean up correctly, there will be FIFOs involved, larger than what the stack allows
The start function will spin up a FreeRTOS thread, the thread is private, give it a stack size of 2048
the on-complete callback must be fired from poll, not the new thread
all actual file and file system operations must be contained within the new thread
the decoding strategy is a bit simpler than the python script

instantiate two AudioFIFO instances, each with 2048 capacity. One for left channel and one for right channel. The rule is: `file_packet_t` has a field `src`. If `src` is an odd number, the data goes into the left channel (this should be mic), if `src` is an even number, the data goes into the right channel (this should be BT).
The FIFO’s watermark feature should be used, both set to 512 samples (1024 bytes).
open the input file

open the output file as “temp.wav”, write a fake WAV file RIFF header inside, once we are finished, this will be overwritten. make sure the file write seek pointer is now at the start of stereo 16 bit PCM data.
Loop
If both FIFOs can accept at least 512 more samples, then decode a packet, and enqueue it into the correct FIFO. (reminder that the maximum samples per packet is 256, although a size of 240 is likely)

Then, remembering that the watermark feature is used, we have three possible conditions: if only the left FIFO allows for dequeuing, if only the right FIFO allows for dequeuing, or if both FIFOs allow for dequeuing.
If both can be dequeued, then do the dequeue 128 samples from each at a time, interleaved into stereo PCM data and written to the output file.
If only one FIFO can be dequeued, then dequeue 128 samples and create stereo PCM data with the other channel silent, write it to the output file
If the file has been completely read to end, then set the watermark to 0 and continue dequeuing until both FIFOs are empty.
Then the loop is finished. Write the RIFF header correctly. Close the file “temp.wav” and then rename it to the proper name.
Set a flag saying it is complete. Break out of the loop. Delete the thread.

Progress report call back is called every 500ms, but also, only check if that time has elapsed every 8 packet being decoded. Progress is reported by the input file seek pointer vs the input file size.
A cancel flag means the temporary file is deleted and then the loop exits, the thread then ends correctly
Implement decryption as needed according to security level
agent_reply: Also added `AudioFifo::setWatermark()` in [AudioFifo.h](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFifo/AudioFifo.h) so the decoder can drop the watermark to zero for EOF draining.
Both builds pass. I left unrelated worktree changes alone, including the existing test/developer-docs files.
